- [ ] `code_review` Jira exclusion is proven by running reconcile, not source grep.

- [ ] Assembles queued changes/stacks into one combined linear chain on the current `main` tip, reusing S2's `/rebase:chain` as the building block for cross-stack chain assembly.

- [ ] Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change), dispatching `gerrit-verify.yaml` against the **real combined-tip change's** actual Change-Id / change-number / patchset (not a synthetic ref); records that run's `run_id` on the batch for S8's confirmation.

- [ ] **FFO TOCTOU re-check:** immediately before the batch is handed off for submit, re-check the combined chain is still on the current `main` tip; if `main` advanced, re-assemble + re-batch — **bounded to `N=5` re-batch attempts**, after which the batch is dropped to the Phase-1 serial path (never hand off a stale tree, never loop unbounded). NOTE: the Phase-1 single-`wipChain` invariant does NOT hold in Phase 2 — this bounded re-batch loop is the mitigation.

- [ ] On green, **hand off to S8** (cast-Verified-per-member → ancestor-atomic submit) via the handoff record `{member_change_ids[], combined_tip_change_id, tested_sha, run_id}`. This story does **not** cast `Verified` and does **not** itself submit.

- [ ] Batch size bounded by a concrete numeric cap **`BATCH_MAX=8`** members per combined-tip CI run (sized to one run's job budget under the GHA concurrency cap); the cap is documented and enforced at assembly (excess changes wait for the next batch).
- [ ] P1-P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool.

- [ ] **CI-flake auto-recheck (before hand-back):** on a post-rebase `Verified -1`, do a **bounded auto-`recheck`** — 1 retry on the SAME patchset — BEFORE recording `ci_failed` and stripping `Autosubmit`; only hand the stack back if it fails again. (The `gerrit-verify` workflow casts a single **undifferentiated** `Verified -1` for all failure modes — there is NO machine-readable infra-vs-test signal in the vote/label/REST API to branch on — so the auto-`recheck` applies to ANY post-rebase `-1`; the single bounded retry is itself what absorbs a transient flake, and a real test-failure that reproduces on the recheck correctly hands back.) **Recheck trigger + state machine (concrete):** the bot triggers CI re-execution by **posting the exact Gerrit comment `recheck`** — which the gerrit-to-platform ChatOps maps to the `verify` workflow (`gerrit_to_platform.ini.template`: `recheck = verify`), re-running `gerrit-verify.yaml` (the bot posts comments as its own Gerrit user; no extra grant needed). It then enters a **`rechecking` in-flight sub-state** (an S3-owned flag on the `wipChain`, NOT a new S2 phase) and reads the vote sequence correctly: `gerrit-verify.yaml`'s `clear-vote` job first **resets `Verified→0`** at run start (GerriScary-safe) — the bot MUST treat this transient `0` as *recheck-running*, NOT as "no result," and MUST NOT re-post `recheck`; it then waits for the terminal vote — `+1` → clear the flag and proceed to submit; `-1` → record `ci_failed` and hand back. The recheck is bounded to **one** post per patchset (the flag prevents a re-post loop). A single flake must not poison an otherwise-green stack (the epic's "zero babysitting" goal).

- [ ] CI-fail: after the auto-recheck still shows `Verified -1`, record a `ci_failed` outcome (derived live from Gerrit), then remove `Autosubmit` from **all changes in the affected stack** (not just the failing change), and stop driving the stack; post NO bot comment (the native `-1` + run logs are the signal — no redundant channel).

- [ ] **Persisted state is a reconstruct-from-Gerrit cache:** persist ONLY `needs_rebase` markers keyed by `change_id + patchset_SHA`; a persisted marker is IGNORED if its patchset SHA no longer matches the change's current patchset in Gerrit. `merged`/`ci_failed`/`review_failed` are NEVER persisted — they are derived live from Gerrit at read time. Keep a rolling per-transition log line for debugging.

- [ ] Rebase-conflict: on `POST /rebase` `409`, record a `needs_rebase` outcome event-driven on that change/stack (NOT a continuously-maintained up-to-date vote — that would churn O(open) per landing), then remove `Autosubmit` from all changes in the stack.

- [ ] **Stack → owner:** the owning agent is the stack's uploader/owner (`owner._account_id` on the tip change); "hand back" = record ONE outcome for the stack + remove `Autosubmit` from every change in it. Never partial-land; never evict a change from within a stack.
- [ ] Never `allow_conflicts` — a `409` from `POST /rebase` routes to the `needs_rebase` path; no conflict-marker patchset is ever created or submitted.

- [ ] **Idempotent ordering under retries/restart:** the outcome is recorded **before** `Autosubmit` removal; label-removal treats `404`/`409` as a no-op; the `stack_id` upsert key makes re-runs overwrite rather than duplicate; on restart an unacknowledged record re-drives the label removal to completion.

- [ ] **Single-instance guard:** the container acquires a POSIX advisory **file lock** (`flock` on the concrete lockfile **`autolander.lock`** at the root of its mounted state volume) on start; a second copy fails to acquire it and exits non-zero. This `flock` is the **sole** single-instance mechanism — do NOT rely on `deploy.replicas: 1` (a Swarm-only key silently ignored by plain `docker compose`).

- [ ] **State volume:** the named volume **`gerrit_autolander`** is declared `external: true` and its subdir `autolander` added to `SITE_SUBDIRS` in `compose-up.sh`; it holds `autolander.lock`, the heartbeat file (S5d), the emergency-stop sentinel (S5d), and `recovery.json` (S5d), so all survive container recreation.

- [ ] **Fresh Verified (per member):** a `Verified +1` on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote). **For a multi-member chain, `rebase:chain` mints a NEW patchset (distinct SHA) per member, so ALL members must carry a fresh `Verified +1` on their own post-rebase SHAs before submit** — submitting while any member lacks a fresh Verified would land an untested tree (violates R5).

- [ ] **Bounded await:** await fresh Verified up to a bounded timeout (default 30 min, configurable, matching `land`'s timeout); on timeout (CI hung), hand the stack back (S2b mechanism) rather than blocking forever. (`LLM-Review` carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` per ADR-0041; `Verified` re-runs on each new patchset.)

- [ ] **Ancestor-atomic submit** = a single `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` on the chain tip. Gerrit submits the chain's members **"Submitted Together"** by relation-chain ancestry (all-or-nothing), reinforced by the **shared topic S3 assigns to the stack** (`stack_id` = the Gerrit topic) with `change.submitWholeTopic` active on this instance. **Partial-land safety (mandatory):** after the submit call, the loop MUST verify **every** stack member reached `MERGED`; if any member is left `open` (a partial land — e.g. members not grouped as expected), it fails LOUDLY (`AUTOLANDER_ERROR` + metric) and hands the stack back rather than proceeding, upholding R3/R5. On full-merge success, close/annotate the associated ticket(s) via `import rebar` ticket ops.

- [ ] **SHA-binding (new threat vector):** at assembly the `{change_id → patchset_SHA}` map is **written durably to the autolander state dir** (a file that survives crash/restart), not held only in memory; before casting `Verified +1` on a member the bot **reads it back** and asserts that member's stored SHA is exactly the one included in the combined-tip run that passed. On crash/restart the batch is re-validated against the stored map before any vote is cast. (GerriScary/copyCondition guards *carried* votes; SHA-binding guards *directly-cast* votes — a distinct vector.) Never a copied or stale vote.

(Parallel-sibling topologies are excluded from a batch; ADR-0040.)

- [ ] **Heartbeat (AC5):** the bot writes a timestamp file in the state volume once per its 15 s poll (S2 cadence); its age is served as `heartbeat_age_s` by S5c's status endpoint. Contract: freshness ≤ 15 s; `land`'s `lander_down` treats age **> 90 s** (6× poll) as down.

- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` marks the container `unhealthy` on a **hang** by stat-ing the **local heartbeat file** and failing if its age exceeds **90 s** (the `lander_down` bound) — it MUST NOT probe Gerrit (a Gerrit outage would flap restarts). **Concrete Docker parameters:** `HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=60s --retries=2 CMD <stat-based check: unhealthy if heartbeat age > 90s>` (interval 30 s × retries 2 → ~90 s to trip, matching the bound; `start-period=60s` avoids boot false-positives). During the ≤120 s drain the bot keeps heartbeating each tick, so the age stays < 15 s and the container remains `healthy` — autoheal is not provoked. The `willfarrell/autoheal` sidecar (S5a) restarts the `autoheal=true`-labeled container on `unhealthy`.

- [ ] **Emergency-stop (prior art: Zuul `pause`):** an emergency-stop **sentinel file** in the state volume (presence = stop; operator creates to pause, removes to resume) transitions the bot to `paused` (the S2 `phase` value) — STOPS consuming new `Autosubmit` changes. Lives on the state volume so a redeploy does not silently un-pause.

- [ ] **Drain-on-shutdown + crash-safe recovery (RELIABILITY FALLBACK):** on SIGTERM the bot's **FIRST action is to write the recovery record** `recovery.json` to the state volume (the `wipChain` snapshot — `change_id`, `chain_member_ids`, `tested_shas`, `phase`, `re_drive_count`, and the S3 handback `acknowledged` flag) — written up-front so recovery is guaranteed no matter what interrupts the drain. It then attempts a graceful finish of an already-near-complete submit + S3 outcome write (prior art: Zuul `graceful`), time-bounded to **120 s**, with the compose service set to **`stop_grace_period: 120s`** so `docker compose stop/down` does not SIGKILL early.

- [ ] **Drain ⇄ autoheal race (must not fight each other):** during the drain the bot **keeps writing its heartbeat each tick** so the HEALTHCHECK stays healthy and autoheal is not provoked into a mid-drain `docker restart` (autoheal's `docker restart` does NOT honour `stop_grace_period`). And because `recovery.json` is written FIRST, even if autoheal *does* restart the bot mid-drain, recovery is still safe (the restart path below reconciles it). Belt-and-suspenders: no interruption can strand a stack.
- [ ] **Restart reconciliation** — on restart the bot reads `recovery.json` + reconciles against LIVE Gerrit and S3's markers BEFORE selecting new work, with a rule for every interruption point:

- [ ] **Heartbeat-freshness proving test:** a test reads `heartbeat_age_s` from the S5c status endpoint (`GET /autolander/status`) while the bot runs and asserts it stays **< 15 s** (fresh); then it stops the heartbeat writer (simulating a hang) and asserts `heartbeat_age_s` crosses **90 s**, that the `HEALTHCHECK` flips the container to `unhealthy`, and that a concurrent `land` returns `lander_down` — bounds-checking the actual measured age, not just the prose contract.

- [ ] **Crash-recovery/idempotency test (the reliability guarantee):** an automated test kills the bot at each `wipChain` phase (`rebasing`, `awaiting_verified`, `submitting`) **and during an in-flight hand-back** (S3 marker `acknowledged=false`), and asserts the restarted instance neither double-submits nor strands a stack, and converges the S3 marker to `acknowledged=true`. A dedicated case asserts the **drain⇄autoheal race is benign**: a SIGTERM followed by an autoheal `docker restart` mid-drain still recovers cleanly (because `recovery.json` is written first).

- [ ] `land <change> --wait` **checks the bot heartbeat FIRST and only sets `Autosubmit` if the heartbeat is fresh** — if the heartbeat is already stale at invocation it returns `lander_down` **without** setting the label (never leave an orphaned `Autosubmit` the down bot will not consume). When fresh, it sets `Autosubmit` **under the agent's OWN Gerrit identity** (a Contributor — requires S1's requester-votable ACL; no bot-side voting endpoint), blocks until terminal, and returns one typed outcome as JSON + a **distinct exit code per outcome** per this table: `0 merged`, `1 needs_rebase`, `2 ci_failed`, `3 review_failed`, `4 not_requested` (land-status only — no `Autosubmit` set and no bot record), `5 abandoned`, `6 lander_down`, `7 error` (any other failure), `75 pending` (land-status only — bot still driving), `124 timed_out`. Every exit code is pinned (no "any nonzero" hole).

- [ ] **`lander_down` (bot-SPOF liveness):** the single-instance bot is a SPOF; if it is down/wedged, `land --wait`/`land-status` must NOT hang to `timed_out`. Both read the bot's liveness from S5's **read-only status endpoint** (which exposes the heartbeat age; the bot writes the heartbeat once per its 15 s poll — see S2); if the reported heartbeat is stale — **older than 90 s** (6× the 15 s poll, so a few consecutive slow iterations / a transient hiccup do NOT trip a false `lander_down`) — they return the distinct `lander_down` outcome + exit code (`6`) IMMEDIATELY (within one `land` poll cycle, not after the 30-min timeout), with the message "lander unavailable — rebase to tip and submit manually (see CONTRIBUTING §2e 'Submit', ADR-0040)." **If the status endpoint is unreachable** (connection refused/timeout), that is treated conservatively as `lander_down` (liveness cannot be confirmed), NOT as `error`. The manual FFO rebase-and-submit is the sanctioned degraded path. *(These operational thresholds — 90 s staleness, 30 s poll, 30 min timeout — are config values coupled to S5's 15 s bot poll; they are read from config, not hard-coded literals, so an S5 poll-rate change can be matched without a code edit.)*

- [ ] `land-status <change>` single-shot variant returns the same enum **plus the non-terminal `pending`** value while the bot is still driving (it does NOT set `Autosubmit` — that is `land`'s job; this resolves the fire-and-forget-then-check-back flow: run `land <change>` once, poll `land-status` which returns `pending` until terminal). `land-status` applies the **same fallback-precedence rule as AC5**. On a change with **no bot record and no `Autosubmit` set**, it returns the native-derived terminal outcome if the change is already merged/abandoned/failing; otherwise `not_requested` (exit `4`) — signalling the agent to run `land` first.

- [ ] **`--wait` termination/timeout:** every 30s the poll loop consults BOTH (a) native Gerrit state via `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` (the `o=DETAILED_LABELS` option is REQUIRED to return per-label vote values; a bare `GET /changes/{id}` returns status + revision only, not label votes) for `merged`/`ci_failed`/`review_failed`/`abandoned` (MERGED / ABANDONED / a failing label), AND (b) the auto-lander's recorded outcome (S3, read via its status query — a read of its state dir / a small status endpoint) for `needs_rebase`, which is NOT derivable from native state (under FFO a behind-tip change is *non-submittable* per ADR-0040, but `submittable=false` alone does not distinguish behind-tip from a failing gate vote); it does NOT rely on `GET /changes/{id}` alone. A Gerrit **`ABANDONED`** status maps to the terminal `abandoned` outcome with its own distinct exit code (`5`); overall timeout 30 min → `timed_out` (exit `124`); transport errors are retried (bounded) before surfacing `error`.

- [ ] **Fallback precedence** for deriving the outcome: (1) the bot's recorded outcome (S3) wins if present; else derive from native Gerrit state — `Verified -1` → `ci_failed`, `LLM-Review -1` → `review_failed`, MERGED → `merged`, `ABANDONED` → `abandoned`; if both `-1` are present with no bot record, `ci_failed` wins. **`needs_rebase` is NOT derivable from native state** — under FFO (ADR-0040) a behind-tip change is *non-submittable* (`submittable=false`), but `submittable=false` alone does not distinguish behind-tip (→ `needs_rebase`, a clean rebase fixes it) from a failing gate vote (→ `ci_failed`/`review_failed`); FFO surfaces the behind-tip case as a "not fast-forward" refusal only on the submit *attempt*, so `needs_rebase` requires the bot's recorded outcome (S3); it never appears via native-state fallback.

- [ ] Command lives in PROJECT tooling (repo CLI/script), NOT `rebar` core; may `import rebar` only for ticket ops (e.g. close the ticket on merge). No Gerrit-specific landing logic in `src/rebar`. **Gerrit REST access reuses this repo's proven STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit-client approach** — the `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` pattern (`urllib` + HTTP Basic auth + XSSI stripping, *"deliberately NO new httpx dependency"*): NOT a new third-party client (not `pygerrit2`) and NOT a from-scratch reimplementation. Packaged as a **thin stdlib helper in `infra/autolander`** (mirrors the proven `urllib`+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency; honors the project-tooling/core boundary; unification behind the platform provider-seam is deferred to idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`). Auth is the **agent's OWN ambient Gerrit HTTP credential** read from the standard place (`.netrc` / the git credential helper the agent already uses to push to Gerrit) and passed as a stdlib `urllib` Basic-auth header — no separate credential-injection mechanism to invent.

Agents depend only on this contract. (The ADR that records the overall auto-lander design and anchors this contract is authored downstream by S6, not here.)

- [ ] **Error metric (LOUD by default):** `observability.sh` publishes `rebar/host:autolander_errors` (`PutMetricData`) from THREE feeders, each a concrete bot-emitted signal: (1) the `AUTOLANDER_ERROR` marker (the loop writes it on **any** unhandled error, then hands the current stack back rather than wedging silently); (2) an `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK` marker (the loop writes it on every stack hand-back — since a hand-back, not the client-side `lander_down`, is what the bot itself can emit); (3) the container `unhealthy` Docker health-state (via `docker inspect --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'`, NOT a journald grep). **Marker format (shared emitter/parser contract):** each marker is a single stdout line = the bare token (`AUTOLANDER_ERROR` / `AUTOLANDER_HANDBACK`) followed by one space and a compact JSON object (e.g. `AUTOLANDER_ERROR {"change":"...","phase":"...","detail":"..."}`), matching the repo's existing `*_ERROR` marker convention; `observability.sh` greps the leading token to count occurrences. Alarm in `monitoring_autolander.tf`.

- [ ] **Stuck-state alarm (high visibility) — as a CloudWatch metric, not an HTTP field:** the loop publishes a `autolander_time_in_phase_seconds` metric (`PutMetricData`, the current `wipChain`'s seconds-in-phase, emitted each poll while non-`idle`); `monitoring_autolander.tf` defines a CloudWatch alarm on THAT metric (alarms watch metrics, not the status-endpoint JSON). This makes a wedged-but-alive bot (a hang autoheal doesn't catch) visible.

- [ ] **Time-to-land metric:** emit `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` (explicit metric name) as **individual `Value` datapoints** per landed change (NOT `StatisticValues` — CloudWatch computes extended-statistic percentiles only from raw `Value` datapoints); p50/p90/p99 come from CloudWatch extended statistics downstream (no hard-coded buckets). **In-session proof** (concrete): a unit test asserts the `PutMetricData` call is built with a single `Value` (not `StatisticValues`) and the `autolander_time_to_land_seconds` name; populated percentiles are an expected post-deploy outcome, not an in-sprint deliverable.

- [ ] The adjudicated corpus is committed under docs/experiments/ (e.g. `code_review_adjudication.jsonl`) with the derivation script/notebook. SCHEMA (define the value vocabulary): each row is `{finding_id: str, criterion: str, impact: float, validity: float, priority: float, adjudication: "block-worthy" | "not-block-worthy" | "ambiguous", evidence: str (the follow-up-fix commit / red-main incident / reviewer rationale grounding the call)}`. SOURCE of validity/impact/priority (code-grounded): each `code_review_result_v1` sidecar finding already carries `validity`, `impact`, AND `priority` — they are the pass-3 decision fields `pass3_decide` writes per finding (decide.py:336-338) and the sidecar preserves in its `blocking`/`advisory` arrays. So the corpus is a STRAIGHT PROJECTION of those stored fields (no reconstruction, no separate validity source needed); the derivation reads the stored `priority` directly and computes precision(block-worthy | priority >= t) over it — priority is exactly what the gate thresholds on. `adjudication` uses exactly that three-value closed vocabulary (block-worthy = a human would want this to block; not-block-worthy = advisory-appropriate; ambiguous = excluded from the precision computation, counted separately).

- [ ] The chosen threshold and its precision-crossover justification (with the 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses) are committed as a REPO ARTIFACT under docs/experiments/ — CI-verifiable that the file exists and names a numeric threshold (not only a ticket comment).

The script itself is additionally proven by a direct run: `.venv/bin/python docs/experiments/derive_code_review_threshold.py --corpus <path>` producing the recorded threshold.

- [ ] **`wipChain` schema:** one in-memory record `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_shas{change_id→sha}, verified_at{change_id→ts}, phase, re_drive_count}` held by the single instance; **`phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle, paused}`** (`paused` = S5 emergency-stop). Single `wipChain` / single instance — never rebases two changes onto the same HEAD concurrently.

- [ ] **`submittable` under FFO:** Gerrit's `submittable` already requires descendant-of-tip (ADR-0040) — a behind-tip change is *non-submittable*, and `submittable=True` means votes-at-MAX AND on-tip. A native `submittable=false` does not say *why* (behind-tip vs failing vote).

- [ ] **FFO TOCTOU guard + bounded re-drive:** immediately before `POST /submit`, re-confirm the tested tree is still submittable (for a chain: the bottom-most member's parent is still `main`'s current tip); if `main` advanced or any tested SHA changed, do NOT submit. Re-drive the same `wipChain` (`phase`→`rebasing`, rebase-to-new-tip → await fresh Verified → re-check), **at most `N=5` times** (`re_drive_count`); a `not fast-forward` submit refusal is handled identically.

- [ ] **Hand-back (prior art: Chromium CQ reset / Zuul dequeue-and-comment):** after `N=5` exhaustion, **remove `Autosubmit` from every stack member + post a Gerrit comment** naming the reason (`needs_rebase` — "main advanced faster than the lander could rebase; rebase onto current main and re-apply Autosubmit") **+ write S3's `needs_rebase` marker**. Label-removal is the "handed back" signal `land` reads. (Same mechanism S3 uses for conflict/CI-fail.)

- [ ] **Attribution algorithm (ONE strategy — isolation, NOT batch-minus-one):** on a combined-batch `Verified -1`, re-run CI on each candidate stack **in isolation on the current `main` tip** (the GHA concurrency cap permits parallel isolated runs); a stack that reproduces `Verified -1` in isolation is the culprit — the isolated re-run both identifies it AND produces the direct per-change `-1`. Batch-minus-one is explicitly NOT used. Never evict a change from within a stack.

- [ ] **All-innocent / flake case:** if a stack **passes** its isolation re-run (no stack reproduces the failure — the batch failure was a flake or a cross-stack interaction), re-batch all stacks once; if the combined run fails **again** while every stack passes isolation, fall back to landing the stacks **serially** (the Phase-1 path) and log the anomaly.

- [ ] **Isolated re-run mechanism:** trigger the isolated CI via `workflow_dispatch` on `gerrit-verify.yaml`, targeting the culprit stack's **real tip change** (its actual Change-Id / patchset), never a synthetic ref.
- [ ] **Multiple culprits (K>1):** hand all K culprit stacks back to their owning agents; re-batch only the remaining innocent stacks into a fresh combined run.
- [ ] Hand the culprit stack(s) back reusing S3's serial `needs_rebase`/`ci_failed` outcome contract; re-batch the innocent stacks. Never partial-land; never break a stack's internal integrity.

- [ ] **Bounded retries:** a per-stack retry counter (max `N=3` re-batch attempts) persisted in the state dir. **The counter increments once per re-batching of that stack** (not per re-vote and not on a re-queue after the agent re-votes `Autosubmit`). On exhaustion the stack is marked with a persisted **`parked` boolean flag** (NOT a new outcome-enum value — the outcome stays `ci_failed`): `Autosubmit` is removed and the parked stack is not re-queued until its agent clears the state by re-voting `Autosubmit`, which resets the counter. Prevents thrash under churn.

- [ ] **Credential:** the Gerrit HTTP token is an SSM **SecureString** at `/rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token` — inside the existing `parameter/rebar/prod/*` prefix the EC2 instance-role IAM policy (`iam.tf` `ssm_params_read`) already grants, so **NO IAM change** (`iam.tf` read-only/unchanged; a `/rebar/autolander/*` path would fall outside the grant). Added via the `ssm.tf` `for_each` set; fetched by `fetch-secrets.sh` using **`get_param` (required/fail-fast — NOT `get_param_optional`)** so a missing token aborts boot rather than silently producing a blank env-var, into the shared `.env` as `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`; never inlined.

- [ ] **Submit is already covered — do NOT add a submit grant.** `RebarBotNava` is already in the `Contributors` group (`setup-project.sh` default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS`), which holds `Submit` on `refs/heads/*`. An All-Projects submit grant would be **eclipsed** anyway: `project.config` sets `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`, so Gerrit ignores inherited submit for that ref. AC = *verify* `RebarBotNava ∈ Contributors` (so `POST /submit` does not 403); no new submit ACL.

- [ ] **Rebase-on-behalf access right (concrete):** grant the **`Rebase`** access right (project.config `[access]` token **`rebase`**, confirmed in this instance's `access-control.html`) to group **`Contributors`** on the `rebar` project's `refs/heads/*` — added to the **rebar project's** `refs/meta/config` via `setup-project.sh`/`project.config` (NOT `All-Projects`, NOT a global `[capability]`, NOT the `[submit] rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` submit-strategy setting). Concrete stanza: `[access "refs/heads/*"] rebase = group Contributors`. The **on-behalf** half needs no extra grant: `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` checks push/upload against the *original uploader*, who already holds it (they created the change). (The bot is already a submitter via Contributors, and "submitters can always rebase" per the ACL docs, so this explicit `rebase` grant is belt-and-suspenders for the REST path.)

- [ ] **Fallback protocol (no silent failure):** the AC3 smoke test is the checkpoint — if the live rebase-on-behalf call 403s despite the `rebase` grant, the 403 body names the missing right; the documented remediation is to add exactly that named right to the same `refs/heads/*` stanza. The plan does NOT proceed to production on a 403.

- [ ] **Tests (AC3) — read-only provisioning verification (no change created/submitted):** `setup-project.sh` remains idempotent (re-run is a no-op). `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` runs three read-only checks against the live instance with the fetched token: (1) **auth** — `curl -s -u "$CRED" .../a/accounts/self` returns 200 (token materialized + valid); (2) **membership** — `RebarBotNava` is a member of `Contributors` (via `.../a/groups/Contributors/members`), so it holds Submit; (3) **ACL present** — `.../a/projects/rebar/access` shows the `rebase` permission granted to `Contributors` on `refs/heads/*`. These prove the *provisioning*; the actual rebase-on-behalf **behavior** (uploader/trailer preserved) is proven by **S2a's** live rebase test and the epic E2E — this task does NOT create/push/submit a change (which would need DCO sign-off + both gate votes and duplicate S2a/S2c). On a missing grant, check (3) fails with the exact missing right, per the fallback protocol above.
